approvedContent.title: ADUO: F1's 2026 engine catch-up system
approvedContent.coreMessage: Explain in simple terms how F1 grants limited extra engine-development opportunities to manufacturers whose combustion engine is behind the benchmark.
verifiedFacts: ADUO means Additional Development and Upgrade Opportunities.
The 2026 to 2030 mechanism is for power-unit manufacturers, not directly for teams or drivers.
The FIA trigger is based on an ICE Performance Index, not total power-unit performance.
At 2% to under 4% behind the leading ICE, a manufacturer can receive one additional upgrade in the current season and one in the following season.
At 4% or more behind, a manufacturer can receive two additional upgrades in the current season and two in the following season.
Energy recovery, deployment, batteries and broader energy management are outside the qualifying ICE measure.
approvedContent.requiredAngle: Make a technical regulation understandable in under 30 seconds for an India-first F1 audience.

clientBrief.audience: Indian F1 fans who may be new to 2026 power-unit rules
clientBrief.offer: independent Formula 1 explainer and news page
boundaries: Do not call ADUO balance of performance or an instant speed boost.
Do not say a team gets ADUO when the manufacturer is the qualifying entity.
